sections.heading: Annual Sensitive Data Training
body: FY 2026 — Required for all staff with access to sensitive systems

sections.heading: What is Sensitive Data?
sections.notes: Emphasize that mishandling any of these creates real harm to real people. The legal consequences for the agency and the individual are severe. This is not a paperwork exercise.

sections.heading: Need to Know Principle
body: Access is granted by job function, not seniority.
Even with general clearance, only access specific records your role requires.
Curiosity is not a need-to-know. Past relationships are not a need-to-know.
Every access is logged. Federal auditors review access logs.
sections.notes: Real example: a state worker in 2023 was prosecuted under IRC 7213A for looking up the tax records of a celebrity case. Curiosity was the only motive. The conviction stood.

sections.heading: Reporting Incidents
body: When you suspect a problem, the clock starts immediately.
You don't need certainty before reporting. Just report.
False alarms are fine. Silence is not.
Contact the Information Security Office through the agency's incident reporting process.

sections.heading: Personal Liability Under IRC
bullets: IRC §7213: up to 5 years federal prison + $5,000 fine
IRC §7213A: up to 1 year prison + $1,000 fine for unauthorized inspection
IRC §7431: civil damages, minimum $1,000 per incident
Plus: termination, loss of clearance, permanent ineligibility for federal work
sections.notes: Make clear: these penalties follow the individual, not the employer. The agency cannot indemnify employees against criminal liability for unauthorized disclosure.
